title: CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY - 211info
text: LIFEWORKS NW ST JOHNS CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. They provide high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. LifeWorks NW provides wraparound services to high-risk families. Services include early childhood intervention and social, emotional, and family support. Also provides mental health services and trauma assessments, traumatic stress expertise, home visitations, parent/infant and parent/toddler classes, art and music therapy, respite child care, and weekly parenting classes using the "Making Parenting a Pleasure" curriculum. Parents/children must be enrolled to receive most services. 8425 N Lombard Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Caregivers with children ages 0-5 and women in their 3rd trimester of pregnancy Serves high-risk families. "High-risk" typically means experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-1pm Email (503) 283-4776 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Program phone (503) 283-4776 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for eligibility screening/assessment and intake appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus lines 4, 17, and 75 Services: Case/Care Management * At Risk Families Children's Out of Home Respite Care * At Risk Families Family Support Centers/Outreach * At Risk Families Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ACTIVE PARENTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT EDUCATION AND SUPPORT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - DAY TREATMENT FOR CHILDREN AND YOUTH MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CARE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PARENT-CHILD INTERACTIVE THERAPY MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - YOUTH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
